chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُلْقِي الدَّوَابَّ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 96 – The one in Ihraam casts off the insects from himself

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/323)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/323)
text: سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ قَمْلَةً وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ بِئْسَ مَا صَنَعَ قَالَ فَمَا فِدَاؤُهَا قَالَ لَا فِدَاءَ لَهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazala Bin Ayoub, from Aban, from Abu Al Jaroud who said,

‘A man asked Abu Ja’farasws about a man who kills the lice while he is in Ihraam. Heasws said: ‘Evil is what he has done’. He said, ‘So what is its expiation?’ Heasws said: ‘There is no expiation for it’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa’id from Fadalah ibn Ayyub from Aban from abu al-Jarud who has said the following: “A man once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram kills a louse. He (the Imam) said, ‘What he has done is awful.’ He then asked, ‘How much is the ransom for it?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘There is nothing for it.’”
